text.title: Automation Robustness and Tool Selection
text.summary: Error handling requirements, monitoring patterns, tool comparison, and antipatterns for workflow design.

rule.statement: Every automation must have: error notification, logging, and a manual fallback path. Silent failures are more damaging than no automation.
rule.rationale: An automation that fails silently (no notification, no log) produces incorrect business state without any signal. A failed lead capture automation means lost leads with no visibility. The manual fallback ensures the process continues even when the automation breaks.

list.item: Error notification: Slack or email alert when automation fails
Logging: record each execution with timestamp and status
Manual fallback: documented steps to run the process without the automation
Testing: run with sample data before going live
Staging period: run automation in parallel with manual process for 1 week
Zapier: best for general integrations, no-code, wide app coverage, $Free-599/mo
Make (formerly Integromat): complex multi-step workflows, visual builder, $Free-299/mo

rule.statement: For self-hosted environments or complex pipelines requiring custom code, use n8n over Zapier. For quick no-code integrations between popular SaaS tools, use Zapier.
rule.rationale: n8n provides full code execution (Python, JavaScript) and self-hosting, eliminating per-task pricing and data privacy concerns. Zapier provides faster setup with a larger pre-built connector library.

antipattern.description: Automating a process before running it manually at least twice and documenting the exact steps.
antipattern.reason: An undefined process cannot be correctly automated. Automating ambiguity at machine speed produces large-scale incorrect outcomes.

antipattern.description: Building complex branching logic into a single automation workflow.
antipattern.reason: Complex automations are fragile and hard to debug. Split complex logic into simpler, chained automations. Each automation should do one thing clearly.

antipattern.description: Setting up an automation and not reviewing it for 3+ months.
antipattern.reason: APIs change, SaaS tools update, data formats shift. An unreviewed automation silently breaks months after deployment. Review all active automations monthly.

text.reference: Registry of active automations with status tracking
